title: Communication Failure
shortDescription: Communication between the main control board and the display board has been lost.
fullDescription: The CF (Communication Failure) error indicates that the main control board and the user interface display board are not communicating. These boards exchange data to coordinate temperature settings, ice maker operation, and user commands. The most common cause is a loose ribbon cable or wiring harness between the two boards.

causes.cause: Loose ribbon cable between main board and display board

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the refrigerator
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the refrigerator for 10 minutes, then plug it back in. This may re-establish communication between the boards.

fixSteps.title: Reseat wiring connections
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the refrigerator. Remove the display panel cover. Locate the ribbon cable or wiring harness connecting the display to the main board. Disconnect and firmly reseat all connectors.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for damage
fixSteps.instruction: Check the ribbon cable for cuts, crimps, or corrosion. The cable runs through the refrigerator door area and can be damaged during door hinge work.

fixSteps.title: Replace display or main board
fixSteps.instruction: If connections are solid, try replacing the display board first (less expensive). If CF persists, the main control board may need replacement.

whenToCallPro: If reseating cables does not resolve CF and you are unsure which board is faulty, a technician with diagnostic tools can determine the failed component.
videoSearchQuery: Whirlpool refrigerator CF communication failure fix

faq.q: Will my food spoil with CF error?
faq.a: The compressor may still run in some cases, but temperature control is compromised. Monitor food temperatures and resolve the issue quickly.

faq.q: Is CF common on Whirlpool refrigerators?
faq.a: CF is moderately common, especially after power surges or if the refrigerator has been moved recently, which can jostle wiring connections.

faq.q: Can I adjust the temperature with CF showing?
faq.a: No. The CF error means the display cannot communicate with the main board, so temperature adjustments will not be accepted.

title: Power Outage Detected
shortDescription: The refrigerator has detected a recent power outage and is alerting the user.
fullDescription: The PO (Power Outage) code is an informational alert that the refrigerator experienced a power interruption. After power is restored, the refrigerator displays PO to alert the user that temperatures may have risen during the outage. The user should check food safety and press a button to acknowledge the alert and resume normal operation.
symptoms: Temperature may be higher than set point
Ice maker may have stopped producing ice

fixSteps.title: Acknowledge the alert
fixSteps.instruction: Press any button on the control panel to dismiss the PO alert. The refrigerator will resume normal cooling operation.

fixSteps.title: Check food safety
fixSteps.instruction: If the power was out for more than 4 hours, check food temperatures. The freezer should be below 0F (-18C) and the fridge below 40F (4C). Discard any food above safe temperatures.

fixSteps.title: Verify cooling operation
fixSteps.instruction: After dismissing the alert, listen for the compressor running. Check that temperatures are decreasing back toward set points over the next few hours.

fixSteps.title: Check power source
fixSteps.instruction: If PO appears repeatedly without known outages, check the outlet connection and circuit breaker for loose connections.

whenToCallPro: PO does not require professional service. If it appears repeatedly without known power outages, an electrician should check the circuit.
videoSearchQuery: Whirlpool refrigerator PO power outage code reset

faq.q: Is PO an error code?
faq.a: No. PO is an informational alert indicating a power interruption occurred. It is not a malfunction.

faq.q: How long can food stay safe without power?
faq.a: A full freezer maintains safe temperatures for approximately 48 hours. A half-full freezer about 24 hours. The refrigerator section stays safe for about 4 hours if the door stays closed.

faq.q: Will the refrigerator restart automatically?
faq.a: Yes. The compressor restarts automatically after power is restored. The PO code is just an alert for the user.

title: Compressor Control Error
shortDescription: The main control board has detected a problem with the compressor circuit.
fullDescription: The PC error code indicates that the main control board has detected an issue with the compressor or its control circuitry. The compressor is not running when it should be, or it is drawing abnormal current. This can be caused by a failed compressor start relay, overload protector, or the compressor itself.

causes.cause: Main control board compressor relay failure

symptoms: Compressor not running (no humming sound from back)

fixSteps.title: Reset the refrigerator
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes, then plug back in. The compressor overload may have tripped due to a momentary issue. Allow 5 minutes after plugging in for the compressor to start.

fixSteps.title: Check start relay
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the refrigerator. Access the compressor at the back lower section. Remove the start relay (plugs onto the compressor terminals). Shake it - if it rattles, the relay is bad.

fixSteps.title: Test start relay and overload
fixSteps.instruction: Use a multimeter to test the start relay for continuity between the S and M terminals. Test the overload protector for continuity. Replace any faulty component.

fixSteps.title: Check compressor windings
fixSteps.instruction: If the relay and overload are good, test the compressor windings directly. Measure resistance between the three compressor terminals. Compare to specifications. Open or shorted windings indicate compressor failure.

partsNeeded.part: Compressor Start Relay and Overload Kit

whenToCallPro: If the compressor windings test bad, compressor replacement is a major repair requiring EPA-certified refrigerant handling. Always use a licensed technician.
videoSearchQuery: Whirlpool refrigerator PC compressor error fix

faq.q: Is a compressor replacement worth it?
faq.a: Compressor replacement costs $400-800+ with labor and refrigerant. If the refrigerator is more than 8-10 years old, a new refrigerator may be more economical.

faq.q: Why does the start relay rattle?
faq.a: The start relay contains a small PTC element that breaks when it fails. A rattling sound confirms the internal component has broken loose.

faq.q: Can I move food to a cooler while diagnosing?
faq.a: Yes. Transfer perishable items to a cooler with ice to prevent spoilage while you troubleshoot.

title: Freezer Sensor Error
shortDescription: The freezer temperature sensor is providing an invalid reading.
fullDescription: Error code E1 indicates that the freezer compartment temperature sensor (thermistor) is reading outside the expected range. The sensor may be open, shorted, or disconnected. Without accurate temperature data, the control board cannot properly regulate the freezer temperature.
symptoms: Freezer too cold or not cold enough
Compressor runs constantly or not at all
Ice cream too hard or melting

fixSteps.title: Reset the refrigerator
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes and plug back in. If the sensor has an intermittent connection, this may clear the error.

fixSteps.title: Locate freezer sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Open the freezer. The sensor is typically clipped to the evaporator cover or ceiling of the freezer compartment. It is a small thermistor on two wires.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the refrigerator. Disconnect the sensor and test with a multimeter. At 0F (-18C), expect approximately 16,000-17,000 ohms. At room temperature, approximately 10,000 ohms.

fixSteps.title: Replace freezer sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the reading is significantly off, open, or shorted, replace the sensor. Unclip the old one, disconnect the connector, and install the replacement.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the sensor does not fix the error, the control board input circuit may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Whirlpool refrigerator E1 freezer sensor error fix

faq.q: Can ice buildup damage the sensor?
faq.a: Yes. Excessive frost or ice around the sensor can physically damage it or insulate it from reading accurate temperatures.

faq.q: What happens if I ignore this error?
faq.a: Without accurate temperature sensing, the freezer may overcool (wasting energy and freezing food solid) or undercool (allowing food to thaw and spoil).

faq.q: Are the freezer and fridge sensors the same part?
faq.a: Often yes, they use the same thermistor part number. However, always verify against your model's parts list.

title: Refrigerator Sensor Error
shortDescription: The fresh food compartment temperature sensor is providing an invalid reading.
fullDescription: Error code E2 indicates that the fresh food (refrigerator) compartment temperature sensor is reading out of range. Similar to E1 but for the refrigerator section, this sensor failure prevents proper temperature regulation in the fresh food compartment.
symptoms: Refrigerator section too cold or too warm
Food freezing in the refrigerator section

fixSteps.title: Reset the refrigerator
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes, then plug back in. Check if the error clears.

fixSteps.title: Locate fridge sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Open the fresh food compartment. The sensor is usually located near the air inlet from the freezer, often behind a cover panel at the top or back of the fridge section.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and test with a multimeter. At 37F (3C), expect approximately 12,000-14,000 ohms.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the reading is out of range, replace the sensor. Disconnect the old one and clip in the replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor tests correctly but the error persists, the control board may need professional attention.
videoSearchQuery: Whirlpool refrigerator E2 fridge sensor error fix

faq.q: Why is my food freezing in the refrigerator section?
faq.a: A faulty sensor may tell the control board the fridge is warmer than it actually is, causing overcooling. Replacing the sensor should fix this.

faq.q: Where exactly is the sensor?
faq.a: It is typically clipped near the air damper or air inlet at the top of the fresh food compartment, sometimes behind a plastic cover.

faq.q: Can I adjust the temperature as a workaround?
faq.a: You can try, but without an accurate sensor the control board does not know the actual temperature, so adjustments may not have the expected effect.

title: Evaporator Fan Motor Error
shortDescription: The evaporator fan motor is not running or not reaching the expected speed.
fullDescription: Error code E5 indicates that the evaporator fan motor is not operating correctly. This fan circulates cold air from the evaporator coils throughout the freezer and refrigerator compartments. Without proper air circulation, the freezer may not maintain temperature and the refrigerator section will warm up, even though the compressor is running.
symptoms: Freezer not cold enough despite compressor running
No air movement felt from freezer vents
Unusual noises or no fan sound from freezer

fixSteps.title: Check for ice buildup
fixSteps.instruction: Open the freezer and remove the rear panel cover to expose the evaporator. Check for ice accumulation around the fan and evaporator coils. If heavily iced, defrost the unit by unplugging for 24 hours.

fixSteps.title: Inspect fan blade
fixSteps.instruction: With the panel removed, try spinning the fan blade by hand. It should spin freely. If it is stuck, check for ice, food debris, or a broken blade.

fixSteps.title: Test fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the fan motor wiring. Test the motor windings with a multimeter for continuity and proper resistance.

fixSteps.title: Replace fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor is defective, remove the mounting clips or screws, disconnect wiring, and install the new motor. Transfer the fan blade to the new motor if it is not included.

repairTime: 15 minutes to 1 hour (plus defrost time if needed)
whenToCallPro: If ice buildup recurs quickly after defrosting, the defrost system may be faulty. A technician should check the defrost heater, thermostat, and timer/board.
videoSearchQuery: Whirlpool refrigerator E5 evaporator fan error fix

faq.q: Why is my freezer cold but my fridge warm?
faq.a: The evaporator fan pushes cold air from the freezer into the fridge. If the fan is not working, the freezer stays cold (it is near the coils) but the fridge warms up.

faq.q: Why does ice build up on the evaporator?
faq.a: Ice buildup usually indicates a defrost system failure. The defrost heater, defrost thermostat, or defrost timer/board may be faulty.

faq.q: Can I defrost the refrigerator faster?
faq.a: You can place bowls of hot water inside and close the door to speed up melting. Do not use a hair dryer or heat gun as this can damage plastic components.

title: Condenser Fan Motor Error
shortDescription: The condenser fan motor is not running or not reaching the expected speed.
fullDescription: Error code E6 indicates that the condenser fan motor has failed or is not running at the proper speed. The condenser fan cools the compressor and condenser coils. Without it, the compressor can overheat and the refrigerant system cannot dissipate heat properly, causing the refrigerator to lose cooling capacity.
symptoms: Compressor cycles on and off frequently
No fan noise from back/bottom of refrigerator

fixSteps.title: Clean condenser coils
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the refrigerator. Remove the lower front grille. Use a coil brush and vacuum to clean the condenser coils of dust and pet hair.

fixSteps.title: Check fan blade
fixSteps.instruction: Access the condenser fan (usually behind the lower rear panel). Check for obstructions - paper, food wrappers, or debris can block the blade.

fixSteps.title: Test fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: Check motor wiring connections. Test the motor windings with a multimeter for continuity. Spin the blade by hand to check for bearing seizure.

fixSteps.title: Replace condenser fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor is defective, remove mounting screws, disconnect wiring, and install the new motor. Transfer the fan blade.

whenToCallPro: If the condenser fan is working but the refrigerator still does not cool, there may be a refrigerant system issue requiring a certified technician.
videoSearchQuery: Whirlpool refrigerator E6 condenser fan error fix

faq.q: How often should I clean condenser coils?
faq.a: Every 6-12 months. More frequently if you have pets that shed. Dirty coils are one of the most common causes of refrigerator cooling problems.

faq.q: Can a failed condenser fan damage the compressor?
faq.a: Yes. Without the condenser fan, the compressor overheats. Prolonged overheating can damage the compressor, which is a much more expensive repair.

faq.q: Where is the condenser fan located?
faq.a: On most Whirlpool models, it is behind the lower rear panel near the compressor. On some models, it may be at the bottom front behind the grille.
